methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $2.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 18 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $900M - $8.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 42.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Axiom Space, a Series G space infrastructure company founded by Michael T. Suffredini and Kam Ghaffarian, is valued at $2.5B post-money on $350M raised. The company operates within a Space & Aerospace sector comp universe of 18 peers across similar valuation tiers, supplemented by five public-market anchors (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and eight private comparables including SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs.

Two valuation methods apply to the analysis. Stage-progression modeling, using a 1.25x step-up multiplier typical of Series G advancement, implies $3.1B. Peer-median analysis across 18 same-sector, similar-tier private and early-public companies yields $1.6B, with the peer range spanning $900M to $8.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiple (42.9x median across sector) cannot be applied due to undisclosed revenue. The peer set includes recent data points such as Relativity Space at $4.2B (private), Astranis at $3.5B (Series D), and Sierra Space at $8.0B (Series C).

Triangulation of the two applicable methods supports a range of $1.4B (low) to $3.6B (high), with a median of $2.4B. The current $2.5B valuation sits slightly above the median, reflecting Series G stage positioning. Key uncertainties center on revenue scale and near-term capital efficiency relative to peers, neither disclosed in available data.
